response.justification: The role's hard requirement is 8 years of C++ programming plus 5 years of large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems work on a cloud compute platform. This candidate's C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments and a university thesis project — nowhere near 8 years. Their depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang web/backend work. They do show genuine distributed-systems experience (high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby) and solid mentoring/leadership signals, but the central C++ and cloud-compute-platform criteria are unmet, making them a poor fit despite a strong general engineering background.
strengths: Real distributed-systems experience: low-latency micro-services handling millions of requests/second
Clear mentoring/leadership track record (workshops, courses, dojos, founder role)
Long, versatile engineering history with strong testing/TDD discipline
concerns: C++ experience is ~6 months, far short of the required 8 years
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure-as-product work
Core stack (TS/Node/DLang) does not align with the C++ systems focus of the role
